title: PHP String Functions
content:
### PHP String Functions

PHP provides extensive string manipulation functions.

### Length and Position

| Function | Description | Example |
|----------|-------------|---------|
| `strlen()` | Get string length | `strlen("Hello")` => 5 |
| `strpos()` | Find position of substring | `strpos("Hello", "ll")` => 2 |
| `strrpos()` | Find last occurrence | `strrpos("Hello lol", "l")` => 7 |
| `substr_count()` | Count occurrences | `substr_count("foo bar foo", "foo")` => 2 |

### Substring and Replace

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `substr($str, $start, $len)` | Extract substring |
| `str_replace($search, $replace, $subject)` | Replace all occurrences |
| `substr_replace($str, $repl, $start, $len)` | Replace substring |
| `trim($str)` | Strip whitespace from both ends |
| `ltrim()` / `rtrim()` | Strip from left / right |

### Case Conversion

```php
<?php
$str = "Hello World";
echo strtolower($str); // hello world
echo strtoupper($str); // HELLO WORLD
echo ucfirst($str);    // Hello World
echo lcfirst($str);    // hello World
echo ucwords($str);    // Hello World
?>
```

### Padding and Formatting

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `str_pad($str, $len, $pad, $type)` | Pad string to length |
| `str_repeat($str, $n)` | Repeat string N times |
| `str_word_count($str)` | Count words |
| `strrev($str)` | Reverse string |

> **Key Point:** String positions in PHP start at 0. If `strpos()` returns `false`, use `!== false` to check (because 0 is a valid position).
codeExample:
"; echo "Position of 'fox': " . strpos($text, "fox") . "
"; echo "Replace: " . str_replace("fox", "cat", $text) . "
"; echo "Substr: " . substr($text, 4, 5) . "
"; echo "Uppercase: " . strtoupper($text) . "
"; echo "Word count: " . str_word_count($text) . "
"; // Check strpos carefully if (strpos($text, "The") !== false) { echo "Found 'The' at the start"; } ?>

quiz.question: What is the return type of strlen()?
Why should you use !== false when checking strpos() result?
options: strpos returns 0 for match at start
